description_long: This object is a handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, featuring a long antenna at the top for signal transmission and reception. The front face displays a small monochrome screen above a numeric keypad, with several labeled buttons for channel selection and communication control. The top section includes two rotary knobs, likely for power/volume and channel selection, as well as an orange emergency button. The sides are equipped with a push-to-talk (PTT) button, a speaker/microphone connector port, and a sturdy belt clip for portability. The back is mostly flat with visible battery contacts at the bottom, indicating a removable battery pack. The device is primarily constructed from black plastic with some rubberized elements for grip and durability, and metallic contacts for connectivity.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, numeric keypad, small display, rotary knobs, and a belt clip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the belt clip and a connector port with protective cover.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the screen, keypad, rotary knobs, and antenna.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a push-to-talk button and additional connector port.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the battery compartment and contact points.
